codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested Rotate Keyset form/review screens, stepper variant, and routes, but the commit contains a blocking verification inconsistency in the new test suite and one navigation gap in the review CTA path.

issues.description: Test expects text "02a3f8...8f2c" that is not rendered by this commit's RotateKeysetFormScreen. The screen data source uses `sharePubkey: "*************"` (src/screens/RotateKeysetScreens.tsx lines 12 and 62), so this assertion cannot pass as written, making the feature's test verification unreliable.

Primary CTA on Review & Generate navigates to `/rotate-keyset/progress`, but this route is not registered in the same commit's router (src/app/App.tsx lines 31-33 only include `/rotate-keyset` and `/rotate-keyset/review`). Clicking it falls through to the catch-all redirect and drops the user to Welcome.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says to use CSS custom properties from paper-tokens.css for colors/fonts, but this feature adds many raw hex/rgba color literals. Clarify whether literal colors are acceptable or tighten guidance/enforcement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/styles/global.css (commit 739ab91) lines 4677-4749 and 4778-4781 add hardcoded values like `#93c5fd`, `#fbbf24`, `#dc2626`, and multiple rgba literals.

summary: Feature scope is mostly implemented, but the committed RotateKeyset screen test suite contains a blocking assertion/component mismatch, and the review CTA targets a route missing in this commit.
